_note: Two-plane decomposition for the COMBINED TKT-0010 (ui-timeline) + TKT-0013 (ui-status-stream) intake / ADR-0122 — shipping a three-tag event-rail family: ui-timeline-item (the shared inert visual atom), ui-timeline (the durable authored-children host, role=list), and ui-status-stream (the live imperative-fed host, role=log). OUTSIDE-IN nodes = the surfaces the three components + their catalog rows + their site pages touch (controls/, a2ui catalog + exemplar, site). INSIDE-OUT actions = what the shipped family must DO. Crossed via hosts. plan:true — each leaf carries the checkable acceptance predicate the builder's tests must satisfy; no source exists yet under controls/{timeline,timeline-item,status-stream}. The item is built FIRST (both hosts depend on it). Reuse-heavy: ui-disclosure (the collapse mechanism), the ui-list role=list precedent, the toast-region imperative-host + MutationObserver precedent, the revealScroll tail-follow discipline, and ndjson-lines (the real-stream browser proof). The ONE novelty leg is the marker-system geometry (F2) — a NEW explicit per-(scale x size) --ui-timeline-* token table, no multiplier (ADR-0038), the ADR-0048 bespoke-geometry precedent. LLD-C(catalog) nodes n25/n26 are a2ui-package build slices dispatched to a2ui-builder after the components are green.

nodes.label: the ui-timeline family (three shipped tags)
ui-timeline-item — the shared inert atom
tag ui-timeline-item, class UITimelineItemElement, folder controls/timeline-item/, extends UIElement, tier pattern, internals.role=listitem
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline-item` — jsdom: `customElements.get('ui-timeline-item')` resolves to a UIElement subclass, NOT instanceof UIFormElement; self-defines on import; the descriptor resolves tier:pattern; `el.internals.role==='listitem'` with NO host role attribute (SPEC-R1)

nodes.label: item props — status/label/description/timestamp/icon/size (defaults ''/''/''/''/''/md)
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline-item` — jsdom: every prop reads its default (status/label/description/timestamp/icon='' , size='md'); status uses prop.enum(['','pending','active','done','error'] as const,''), size uses prop.enum(['sm','md','lg'] as const,'md'); each reflects on set (SPEC-R2 AC1/AC2)

nodes.label: item props negative controls — fail-open enums + NO variant/color prop + NO timestamp codec
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline-item` — jsdom: an out-of-vocabulary status/size via setAttribute resolves to values[0] (''/md), never a crash; a grep of static props confirms NO variant/color prop (state rides status) and timestamp is a plain string (no codec) (SPEC-R2 AC2/AC3)

nodes.label: anatomy — host-as-grid marker cell (::before dot + ::after connector) + content roles label/description/timestamp/trailing; slotted marker suppresses the dot; empty roles collapse
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline-item` + `npm run test:browser timeline-item` — an item with label/timestamp only renders the dot+connector and the empty description/trailing roles occupy no space; icon='' + a [data-role=marker] child (or icon set) suppresses the ::before dot; the wrapper-trap regression holds (a wrapped consumer [data-role=label] suppresses the default stamp, no duplicate — the adia timeline.test.js lesson) (SPEC-R3)

nodes.label: status states + non-color signifiers (ADR-0057) — distinct marker SHAPE per pending/active/done/error, forced-colors-legible, reduced-motion-safe
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser timeline-item` (Chromium+WebKit) — each of pending/active/done/error renders a marker that differs by SHAPE not only color (a per-state computed-style/structural probe, NOT a pixel diff); under forced-colors:active every status stays distinguishable (shape/border survives, fill is not the sole cue); under prefers-reduced-motion an active pulse/spinner is static (SPEC-R4)

nodes.label: collapsible detail — ui-disclosure reuse (open + toggle), one nesting level, NO new event name
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline-item` — an item with detail content composes a ui-disclosure (import '../disclosure/disclosure.ts'); toggling emits exactly one `toggle` (allowlisted) and no other event; the adia timeline-toggle custom name is NOT used; nesting is flat+1 (deeper is a fenced non-goal) (SPEC-R5)

nodes.label: marker-system CSS + the NEW --ui-timeline-* token chain (F2) — grid rail, ::before dot / ::after connector in the gutter column
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline-item` + `npm run test:browser timeline-item` — timeline-item.css scopes to :where(ui-timeline-item), declares the --ui-timeline-{marker-box,dot-size,icon-size,connector-width,gutter,row-gap} chain, lays out grid [gutter][content 1fr][aside auto] so stacked items align to one axis; content text reads --md-sys-typescale-* not the marker table; a forced-colors block is present (SPEC-R13 shape, SPEC-R15)

nodes.label: ui-timeline — the durable host

tag ui-timeline, class UITimelineElement, extends UIContainerElement, tier pattern, internals.role=list; authored-children DOM-order ingress; terminal-connector suppression; the STATIC negative control
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline` + `npm run test:browser timeline` — `customElements.get('ui-timeline')` is a UIContainerElement subclass, tier:pattern, `el.internals.role==='list'`, no host role attr; three authored ui-timeline-item children render in DOM order (no auto-sort) and the last item's connector is suppressed (data-last, re-marked on childList change via MutationObserver); a grep of timeline.ts finds NO append/update/finalize, NO tail-follow, NO live-region role (the static negative control) (SPEC-R6)

nodes.label: timeline props — size (first-class F2) + label→internals.ariaLabel; events:[]; NO variant/data/orientation prop
nodes.accept: `npm test timeline` — jsdom: size reads 'md' unset, reflects, fails open; a non-empty label sets internals.ariaLabel (cleared to null on ''); the host dispatches NO event on any interaction (events:[]); a grep confirms no variant/data/orientation prop (SPEC-R7)

nodes.label: ui-status-stream — the live host
tag ui-status-stream, class UIStatusStreamElement, extends UIContainerElement, tier pattern, internals.role=log (POLITE live region via internals.role, the toast precedent)
nodes.accept: `npm test status-stream` — jsdom: `customElements.get('ui-status-stream')` is a UIContainerElement subclass, tier:pattern, `el.internals.role==='log'`, no host role attr, never assertive by default; the SAME content authored under ui-timeline (role=list) vs appended under ui-status-stream (role=log) yields a DIFFERENT ARIA role — the mechanical proof the two are distinct controls, not one posture (SPEC-R8)

nodes.label: imperative data contract — append(entry):UITimelineItemElement + keyed update(key,patch) + finalize(); NO bound data prop; NO transport/parsing
nodes.accept: `npm test status-stream` — append({key:'a',status:'active',label:'search'}) renders a ui-timeline-item and returns it; update('a',{status:'done'}) transitions the SAME element in place (keyed identity, no second element); update('missing',...) is a silent no-op (no throw); update('a',{text:'…'}) grows the entry text in place; a grep confirms status-stream.ts has NO fetch/ReadableStream/readNdjsonLines (the consumer owns transport; text is never tokenized/parsed) (SPEC-R9)

nodes.label: tail-follow scroll + the stick-to-bottom guard (owns its scroll region)
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser status-stream` (Chromium+WebKit) — appended at the bottom, the viewport follows the newest entry's end; scrolled UP (not at bottom), an append does NOT move the viewport (the guard holds), and returning to the bottom resumes follow; under prefers-reduced-motion the follow is an instant jump; the host owns overflow-block:auto (SPEC-R10)

nodes.label: the completion invariant — finalize() marks still-pending/active entries TRUNCATED (fail-closed), never a forever-spinner
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser status-stream` — an active entry then finalize() renders that entry as truncated/interrupted (a distinct non-color-only affordance, ADR-0057), not active; a torn stream (transport throws mid-entry) shows the affected entry truncated and NO entry shows 'still working' (SPEC-R11)

nodes.label: status-stream props — size (F2) + label; collapse of resolved entries emits toggle; NO other event name
nodes.accept: `npm test status-stream` — jsdom: size reads 'md' unset/reflects/fails open; collapse of a resolved entry emits exactly one `toggle` and no other event; streamed text/state announcements ride the role=log live region, not events (SPEC-R12)

nodes.label: shared geometry, whole-shape, tokens, descriptors
the marker-system geometry under [size] x [scale] — explicit per-(scale x size) integer rows, NO multiplier (F2 novelty leg)
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser timeline-item` — at a fixed [scale], the three size values yield marker-box/dot/connector-width/gutter/row-gap equal to the EXPLICIT integers the LLD/CSS pin (a geometry-under-[size] probe asserting exact values) AND the probe does NOT assume adjacent tiers are all-distinct (the ADR-0038 stepping law); [density=spacious] vs compact widens/narrows row-gap only (marker-box/dot/gutter density-invariant); a [scale] change shifts the register with no size-prop change (SPEC-R13)

nodes.label: whole-shape proof per host — a populated rail with aligned markers, not a collapsed sliver
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser {timeline,status-stream}` — a populated ui-timeline in a realistic flex/grid container renders a real rail (markers vertically aligned to one axis, content columns non-collapsed, real width/height) cross-engine; a single bare ui-timeline-item renders at a sensible minimum (marker + one text line), never a 0-width row (the ui-slider whole-shape lesson) (SPEC-R14)

nodes.label: token surface + forced-colors per component (family-coherence)
nodes.accept: `npm test` (family-coherence.test.ts) — each of the three ships a single {name}.css declaring only its --ui-{name}-* roles and consuming only own-union-allowlisted tokens, with a `@media (forced-colors: active)` block; extends ∈ the base ladder; events ⊆ the six; barrel + component-styles registration present (SPEC-R15)

nodes.label: three {name}.md descriptors — tier:pattern, extends, role (internals) listitem/list/log, events, slots/parts (marker+content roles), keyboard map
nodes.accept: `npm test` (family-coherence A-group) — each descriptor exists, tag/class/folder aligned, tier:pattern, extends ∈ BASE_CLASSES (UIElement/UIContainerElement), face.formAssociated:false, the item's keyboard/interaction map documented, and the item's :state(truncated)/customStates + slotted roles declared (SPEC-R16)

nodes.label: descriptor ↔ props + descriptor ↔ source trip-wires (all three)
nodes.accept: `npm test {timeline-item,timeline,status-stream}` — compareDescriptorToProps is green per component (attributes[] mirrors static props 1:1, red on any drift); compareDescriptorToSource confirms every CSS-styled slot/role and used :state() is documented (the text region + data-truncated state declared) (SPEC-R16)

nodes.label: catalog dispositions (a2ui build slices)
Timeline + TimelineItem default-catalog rows (EMITTABLE, F5) — a2ui build slice
nodes.accept: `npm test` (a2ui catalog-coverage) — the moment the descriptors ship, Timeline/TimelineItem enter FLEET_TYPES (ADR-0087); real catalog rows map the item attrs (status/label/description/timestamp/icon/size) + a children item list (the Row/adia model), so the coverage gate stays green WITHOUT an allowlist entry for these two; an agent-activity exemplar composing a Timeline with TimelineItem children validates clean and renders through the real types (SPEC-R17)

nodes.label: StatusStream EXCLUSION_ALLOWLIST entry (NOT emittable, F5) — a2ui build slice
nodes.accept: `npm test` (a2ui catalog-coverage + residue guard) — StatusStream enters FLEET_TYPES as an EXCLUSION_ALLOWLIST member with the ADR-0122 F5 reason (a consumer-owned imperative streaming host, the Toast/ToastRegion cl.6 precedent); the coverage gate's only residue is that one reasoned allowlist entry; a payload attempting to emit StatusStream is absent from the emittable set (SPEC-R17)

nodes.label: site surfaces + gates
site pages — timeline-{doc,demo} + status-stream-{doc,demo}; the status-stream demo drives a recorded stream
nodes.accept: `npm test` (site-coverage.test.ts) — the tier:pattern required {doc,demo} set exists for ui-timeline AND ui-status-stream; *-doc are descriptor-derived API pages; timeline-demo renders a populated durable chronology (real items, states, a collapsible detail); status-stream-demo renders a live tail-following strip fed by a real recorded stream (SPEC-R18)

nodes.label: representative gallery/preview specimens (per host) — the example-builder concern
nodes.accept: `npm test` (site gallery/preview suites) — each host's preview specimen is a POPULATED multi-item rail with realistic states (not one lorem item — the whole-shape/representative-specimen law); the knob config surfaces size + the item status states as one knob per prop of the right type (SPEC-R18)

nodes.label: the REAL-stream browser proof (SPEC-R19) — ui-status-stream fed the in-repo arena NDJSON via readNdjsonLines (INSTRUMENT-BRIDGE)
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser status-stream` (Chromium+WebKit) — recorded NDJSON lines fed line-by-line through append/update/finalize: the strip appends new entries in order, a keyed update transitions an entry in place, tail-follow tracks the newest (with the stick-to-bottom guard), and a feed truncated mid-entry renders that entry truncated (SPEC-R11); runnable with NO live key (the recorded stream is the fixture) (SPEC-R19)

nodes.label: jsdom probes — props reflect/fail-open, ARIA-via-internals, no-event negative control, keyed update identity
nodes.accept: `npm test {timeline-item,timeline,status-stream}` green — reflection round-trips + fail-open for every enum prop; role/label via internals only (no host attribute); the no-event negative control bites on both hosts; append/update keyed identity (update mutates the same element, not a new one)

nodes.label: cross-engine browser probes — geometry-under-[size] + whole-shape + status signifiers + forced-colors + tail-follow + completion
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser {timeline-item,timeline,status-stream}` (Chromium+WebKit) — the geometry-under-[size] integer proof, the aligned-marker whole-shape rail, the per-status distinct-SHAPE signifiers, the forced-colors legibility, the tail-follow + stick-to-bottom guard, and the completion-invariant truncation — all browser-only (jsdom-green ≠ done; the checkbox/date-time precedent)

nodes.label: barrel/exports/size integration (ADR-0080 marginal)
nodes.accept: `npm run check && npm test` green (barrels.test.ts, exports drift gate) for all three tags; `npm run size` run by hand (ADR-0040 §3) — the family marginal measured and pinned if it changes the total materially

nodes.label: independent component-reviewer GO per host before commit
nodes.accept: a fresh-context component-reviewer pass returns GO for ui-timeline-item, ui-timeline, and ui-status-stream against the descriptor + anatomy + geometry + contract fidelity (the standing per-control-wave DoD); generator ≠ critic

actions.label: identify the shared item as a Pattern-class UIElement listitem in the controls/ tier
carry the item's status/label/description/timestamp/icon/size props with first-class size geometry
reject out-of-vocabulary enums fail-open and own no variant prop and no timestamp codec
render the marker + connector + content-role anatomy, collapsing empty roles and honoring a slotted marker
carry a distinct non-color marker shape per status state, forced-colors-legible and reduced-motion-safe
reveal an optional collapsible detail by reusing ui-disclosure, emitting only toggle
size its marker rail from the new explicit --ui-timeline-* grid tokens
identify the durable host as a role=list container ingesting authored children in DOM order, statically
carry the durable host's first-class size and accessible name, emitting no events
identify the live host as a role=log polite live region, distinct from the durable list
expose an imperative append + keyed update + finalize contract owning no transport or parsing
tail-follow the newest entry with a stick-to-bottom guard, owning its scroll region
hold the completion invariant — mark unresolved entries truncated, fail-closed
carry the live host's first-class size and collapse-toggle, emitting no other event
resolve the marker system under [size] x [scale] from explicit integer rows, no multiplier
render each host as a real aligned-marker rail, never a collapsed sliver
declare its --ui-{name}-* token chain and stay legible under forced-colors
record each public surface truthfully in a tier:pattern descriptor
keep each descriptor in bijection with its live props and source
enter the generative-UI catalog as emittable Timeline + TimelineItem types
stay out of the emittable set as an allowlisted StatusStream host
give the fleet API + demo pages, the status-stream demo driving a recorded stream
show each host's real job in a representative populated preview specimen
prove the live host against a REAL streamed source cross-engine
prove props/ARIA/no-event/keyed-identity under jsdom
prove geometry/whole-shape/signifiers/forced-colors/tail-follow/completion cross-engine
stay within the family size budget, exported through the public barrel
pass independent review per host before shipping
